players.name: Fahad Talib
players.bio: Al-Talaba's dependable last line of defence, Fahad Talib arrives at his first World Cup with 21 caps and a calm, authoritative presence between the posts. A product of Iraqi club football, he's earned his shot on the grandest stage.
players.facts: At 31, Talib finally gets his World Cup moment — proof that patience is a goalkeeper's greatest virtue.

players.name: Rebin Sulaka
players.bio: A veteran of 56 caps, Rebin Sulaka is one of Iraq's most experienced defenders heading into 2026. The Port right-back has been a reliable presence in the Lions of Mesopotamia's backline for over a decade of international duty.
players.facts: With 56 caps and just 1 goal, Sulaka's attacking threat is best described as a pleasant surprise rather than a habit.

players.name: Hussein Ali
players.bio: One of Iraq's most exciting young defenders, Hussein Ali plies his trade in Poland with Pogoń Szczecin, bringing European experience to a youthful Lions backline. At just 24, this World Cup could be his definitive international coming-of-age.
players.facts: Ali is one of very few Iraqi internationals playing in the Polish Ekstraklasa — Baghdad to Szczecin is quite the journey.

players.name: Zaid Tahseen
players.bio: Zaid Tahseen has earned his stripes with Uzbekistan's Pakhtakor while racking up 28 caps for Iraq. A composed centre-back with an eye for goal, the 25-year-old brings grit and ambition to Iraq's defensive setup at his first World Cup.
players.facts: Tahseen plays his club football in Tashkent — making him one of the few Iraqis with Uzbek league experience on the world stage.

players.name: Akam Hashim
players.bio: Al-Zawraa stalwart Akam Hashim is a physical and determined defender who has grown steadily into the Iraqi national team setup. With 14 caps and a goal to his name, the 27-year-old offers energy and aerial presence from the back.
players.facts: Hashim has scored more international goals (1) than many forwards with twice his caps. Defenders who chip in — love to see it.

players.name: Manaf Younis
players.bio: Al-Shorta's Manaf Younis is a seasoned left-sided defender with 34 caps and a steady temperament to match. A dependable domestic professional who has cemented his place in Iraq's defensive plans through consistency and discipline.
players.facts: Younis has represented Iraq more than 30 times while never leaving the Iraqi Premier League — loyalty at its finest.

players.name: Youssef Amyn
players.bio: Youssef Amyn is one of Iraq's brightest young midfield talents, thriving in Cyprus with AEK Larnaca at just 22. Two international goals from 27 caps show his attacking instincts, and 2026 could be the tournament that puts him on the map.
players.facts: Born in 2003, Amyn could play at a World Cup before he's old enough to have watched one he can fully remember.

players.name: Ibrahim Bayesh
players.bio: Ibrahim Bayesh is the heartbeat of Iraq's midfield — 76 caps at just 26 is extraordinary by any measure. The UAE-based midfielder reads the game with maturity beyond his years and chips in with goals too, bagging 8 for his country.
players.facts: Bayesh has 76 international caps at age 26. For context, many players retire before reaching that total.

players.name: Ali Al-Hamadi
players.bio: Ali Al-Hamadi has been turning heads in the English Championship with Luton Town, bringing pace and clinical finishing to Iraq's attack. The 24-year-old's 5 goals in 20 caps make him one of the most exciting forwards in the squad.
players.facts: Al-Hamadi is one of the rare Iraqi footballers to have played regularly in English league football — Luton's loss is Iraq's gain.

players.name: Mohanad Ali
players.bio: Iraq's talisman and top scorer, Mohanad Ali carries the weight of Mesopotamian footballing hopes with 27 goals in 72 caps. The No.10 is direct, creative, and lethal — a danger every time he picks up the ball in the final third.
players.facts: Mohanad Ali averages a goal roughly every 2.7 games internationally. Not bad for someone still only 25 years old.

players.name: Ahmed Qasem
players.bio: Ahmed Qasem makes history as one of Iraq's first MLS-based internationals, playing for Nashville SC. With only 3 caps to his name, the 22-year-old is a World Cup wildcard — raw, hungry, and ready to make his mark in North America.
players.facts: Qasem plays his club football in Nashville — meaning he might already know his way around the 2026 host cities better than most.

players.name: Jalal Hassan
players.bio: The most capped goalkeeper in this squad at 102 appearances, Jalal Hassan is an Iraqi football institution. The Al-Zawraa veteran is a calm, commanding presence and brings World Cup experience and leadership that money simply cannot buy.
players.facts: With 102 caps, Hassan has kept goal for Iraq through more campaigns than most players have birthdays. A true Lions legend.

players.name: Ali Yousif
players.bio: Ali Yousif is a seasoned club campaigner with Al-Talaba who has found international opportunities harder to come by, with just 7 caps at age 30. A reliable option off the bench, he brings experience and composure when called upon.
players.facts: Yousif is 30 with 7 caps — a reminder that World Cup squads reward quality over quantity, and patience over everything.

players.name: Zidane Iqbal
players.full_name: Zidane Ammar Iqbal; زيدان عمار إقبال
players.bio: Born in Manchester to Iraqi parents, Zidane Iqbal — yes, named after *that* Zidane — came through Manchester United's academy before joining Utrecht. A technically gifted central midfielder, at 23 he's one of Iraq's most thrilling prospects.
players.facts: Zidane Iqbal was literally named after Zinedine Zidane. No pressure, then — just channel your namesake and you'll be fine.

players.name: Ahmed Maknzi
players.full_name: Ahmed Hassan Maknzi Salman Al-Lami Al-Reeshawee; أحمد حسن مكنزي
players.bio: Baghdad-born Ahmed Maknzi is a composed left-back with Al-Karma who has begun to establish himself in Iraq's defensive setup with 7 caps. Still only 24, the full-back offers energy down the flank and is one for the future.
players.facts: Maknzi plays for Al-Karma — a club whose name literally means 'the generous one.' Giving away fouls is not encouraged.

players.name: Amir Al-Ammari
players.full_name: Amir Fouad Abboud Al-Ammari; أمير فؤاد عبود العماري
players.bio: Born in Jönköping, Sweden, Amir Al-Ammari chose Iraq over the Scandinavians and hasn't looked back. With 51 caps and a composed defensive midfield game honed at Polish side Cracovia, he's the engine room Iraq depend on.
players.facts: Al-Ammari was born in Sweden, developed in Europe, and plays his heart out for Iraq. Football's diaspora stories don't get better.

players.name: Ali Jasim
players.full_name: Ali Jasim Elaibi Al-Tameemi, علي جاسم لعيبي التميمي
players.bio: A product of Rusafa in Baghdad, Ali Jasim is a remarkably young player who already has 36 caps at just 22. The left-sided player at Al-Najma is direct and tenacious, and 2026 could be the World Cup that defines his generation.
players.facts: Ali Jasim has 36 international caps and hasn't yet turned 23. At this rate he could be Iraq's all-time cap leader by 30.

players.name: Aymen Hussein
players.full_name: Ayman Hussein Ghadhban Al-Mafraje; أيمن حسين غضبان المفرجي; Abu Tubar
players.bio: Aymen Hussein is the Lions of Mesopotamia's record-breaking centre-forward — 33 goals in 95 caps is the stuff of legend. Born in Kirkuk, the Al-Karma striker is Iraq's greatest goalscoring hope and a genuine World Cup threat at 30.
players.facts: Aymen Hussein has scored 33 goals for Iraq — more than one every three games. The Lions' all-time great marksman.

players.name: Kevin Yakob
players.full_name: Kevin Enkido Yakob; كيفن إنكيدو يعقوب
players.bio: Göteborg-born Kevin Yakob brings Scandinavian steel and technical quality to Iraq's midfield, playing his club football with AGF in Denmark. Still relatively fresh with 9 caps, the 25-year-old is one of 2026's more intriguing squad members.
players.facts: Yakob was born in Sweden, plays in Denmark, and represents Iraq. Three countries, one footballer — globalisation in action.

players.name: Aimar Sher
players.full_name: Aimar Hazar Sher, أيمار هزار شير
players.bio: Kirkuk-born Aimar Sher — named, it seems, with Scandinavian flair in mind — plays for Sarpsborg 08 in Norway. A technically tidy central midfielder with 7 caps, the 23-year-old is developing steadily and is one to watch in 2026.
players.facts: Aimar Sher: born in Kirkuk, plays in Norway, and has a name that sounds like a Scandinavian pop star. Football's a global village.

players.name: Marko Farji
players.bio: Norwegian-born Marko Farji is a pacy left winger who has caught the eye with Venezia in Italy's Serie A. With 12 caps and a flair for the unexpected, the 22-year-old Grimstad native is Iraq's most exciting wide option at this tournament.
players.facts: Farji grew up in Grimstad, Norway's sunniest town, and now lights up Serie A. From the Norwegian coast to the Adriatic — class.

players.name: Ahmed Basil
players.full_name: Ahmed Basil Fadhil Al Fadhli; أَحْمَد بَاسِل فَاضِل الْفَضْلِيّ
players.bio: Baghdad-born Ahmed Basil is a composed and experienced shot-stopper with Al-Shorta who brings domestic pedigree to Iraq's goalkeeping department. With 16 caps, the 29-year-old is a solid third option with ambitions of more.
players.facts: Basil plays for Al-Shorta, Iraq's oldest professional club — keeping goal for a side steeped in over 80 years of history.

players.name: Merchas Doski
players.full_name: Merchas Ghazi Salih Doski; ميرخاس غازي صالح دوسكي
players.bio: Hannover-born Merchas Doski is a marauding left-back at Czech giants Viktoria Plzeň, bringing European pedigree to Iraq's backline. With 31 caps and Champions League qualifying experience, Doski is one of Iraq's most well-travelled defenders.
players.facts: Doski plays Champions League qualifying football with Plzeň — not many Iraqi left-backs can say that on their CV.

players.name: Zaid Ismail
players.full_name: Zaid Ismail Al-Dulaimi; زيد اسماعيل الدليمي
players.bio: Diyala-born Zaid Ismail is a disciplined defensive midfielder at Al-Talaba who is still building his international career with 6 caps. At 24, the young anchorman offers Iraq options in front of the back four and could grow into this tournament.
players.facts: Ismail has 6 caps — making his World Cup call-up one of the most dramatic squad inclusions in Iraq's recent history.

players.name: Mustafa Saadoon
players.full_name: Mustafa Saadoon Abbood Al-Korji; مصطفى سعدون
players.bio: Mustafa Saadoon is a tenacious right-back at Al-Shorta with 17 caps and an appetite for overlapping runs. The 25-year-old adds width and defensive solidity to Iraq's right flank, and the World Cup stage is a fitting reward for his progress.
players.facts: Saadoon hails from Corgi, Iraq — a place that shares a name with the Queen's favourite dog breed. Quite the fun fact at parties.

players.name: Frans Putros
players.bio: Born in Aarhus, Denmark, Frans Putros has taken a winding path to the World Cup — now representing Iraq at 32 with Persib in Indonesia. His 28 caps prove his commitment, and his experience across three continents makes him uniquely valuable.
players.facts: Putros plays in Indonesia, was born in Denmark, and represents Iraq. His passport has probably seen more stamps than most diplomats.
